title: 5 takeaways as Red Sox rally then fall, 4-3, to Blue Jays to seal sweep

title: Smith & Wesson reports surge in firearms sales
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News The former Springfield-based firearms maker reported a sharp increase in quarterly sales despite a broader decline in background checks nationwide.
Attendees look at a display of Smith and Wesson handguns during the NRA Annual Meeting.
(Photo by Justin Sullivan/Getty Images) Even as firearm background checks continue to decline from record highs seen during the pandemic, Smith & Wesson is reporting a sharp increase in sales.

title: Enjoy the Red Line now, a big chunk of it will shut down after the World Cup
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News The Red Line will be closed between Alewife and Park Street for 10 days in late July.
Red Line service will be suspended between Alewife and Park Street for 10 straight days this summer.
David Ryan/Boston Globe Boston is currently teeming with visitors in town for the World Cup, who are making frequent use of the city’s transit infrastructure.

title: Lindsay Clancy back in court as lawyers argue whether jury should hear husband’s ‘horrific’ 911 call

title: SJC blocks tax cut measure from appearing on November ballot
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Local News “Because this summary materially misstates the true scope of the proposal, it is unfair,” the SJC wrote.
The Supreme Judicial Court on Wednesday April 8, 2026.

(Pat Greenhouse/ The Boston Globe Staff) By Annie Jonas June 18, 2026 | 11:35 AM 2 minutes to read Massachusetts’ highest court on Thursday barred a proposed ballot question aimed at lowering the state’s income tax rate from appearing before voters in November, ruling that the measure’s official summary was misleading.

title: ‘Not as good as Boston’: Scottish fans’ humorous review of New York City during World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Morning Sports Update "We've got to get back to the good American side.
" Scottish soccer fans of the Tartan Army during the march to Fenway Park on June 14.

AP Photo/Mark Stockwell By Hayden Bird June 18, 2026 | 11:29 AM 2 minutes to read The Scots are already loyal Bostonians: As if the Tartan Army hadn’t already endeared themselves enough to Boston, they’ve now started to weigh in on the eternal local rivalry with New York.
